body.objective: Repair Codex/OpenCode runtime contracts so loop-service and execution-engine use the same probed CLI contract before worker execution.
constraints: Codex must use exec --json --cd.
OpenCode must use run --format json --dir.
Runtime drift must block execution with explicit evidence.
Do not start live workers in this goal.
acceptance_criteria: CodexExecutor uses current Codex flags and has focused tests.
Shared runtime contract probe covers Codex, OpenCode and mock.
Runtime contract response includes command, version, required flags, status, reason, evidence and checked_at.
Tests cover available, drifted and unavailable runtime contracts.

body.objective: Implement G15 enforcement repairs: OKF path allowlist, scoped permissions, unspoofable governance refs, runner-owned manifests and typed claim relationships.
constraints: No arbitrary OKF filesystem path.
No generic create:task permission for intelligence writes.
No request boolean may satisfy enforceable governance.
No public API may assert completed runner facts.
acceptance_criteria: OKF drift and sync accept only configured root ids or allowlisted canonical paths.
Capabilities, claims, governance, runner manifests and swarm proof actions use scoped permissions.
Governance enforcement resolves persisted maker/checker/security/quorum/human refs.
Claim ledger supports typed predicate/object/scope and explicit relationship edges.
Tests cover path escape, spoofed governance, spoofed manifest and same-subject non-contradiction.

body.objective: Add OpenCode MCP and skill health reporting that detects database locks, missing config, per-agent MCP exposure and skill permissions without credential leakage.
constraints: Do not delete or rewrite OpenCode state automatically.
Do not persist OAuth tokens, API keys or authorization headers.
Do not globally enable heavy MCP servers as a shortcut.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode MCP health reports ok, locked, unconfigured, unavailable or error.
database is locked is classified with remediation.
opencode.jsonc inspector reports missing mcp, tools, agent and permission.skill sections.
Skill permissions and MCP config become capability candidates only.
Tests cover locked DB output, missing config, per-agent recommendation and credential-value redaction.

body.objective: Model OpenAI Agents SDK, OpenAI Skills and OpenAI MCP/connectors as governed capabilities without pretending they are local workers.
constraints: OpenAI Agents SDK is an orchestration candidate, not an automatic local worker runtime.
OpenAI Skills are privileged until reviewed.
Remote MCP/connectors require approval and authorization refs.
Do not persist raw tokens or authorization headers.
acceptance_criteria: Capability descriptors exist for OpenAI Agents SDK, OpenAI Skills and OpenAI MCP/connectors.
Descriptors cannot route local workers without validated adapter proof.
Remote tool capabilities require approval refs and redacted auth refs.
Tests cover non-routing candidates and missing approval blocks.

body.objective: Build the governed proof-run service that creates real live Djimitflo records from capability to panel to claim to goal to loop to worker evidence to memory candidate.
constraints: Every proof-created record must include proof_run_id, rollback_group and demo_record marker.
Mock runtime must run before real Codex/OpenCode.
Auto-start the mock proof path so live output is created.
Release actions require an explicit operator command outside the proof runner.
Rollback must affect only proof-run demo records.
acceptance_criteria: Mock proof run creates required minimum records across goals, loop_runs, worker_leases, capabilities, claims, manifests, panels, reviews, work_items, memory_candidates, traces and checkpoints.
Proof run records missing evidence explicitly.
Operational demo memory candidate is created and may be marked proof-promoted when rollback-scoped.
Service tests cover creation, missing evidence, forbidden action guard and rollback scoping.

body.objective: Expose proof runs through authenticated API and CLI scripts so reviewers can start, inspect and rollback proof output.
constraints: API requires auth and proof-run permission.
CLI uses accelerated proof defaults.
Rollback requires proof_run_id.
Release and deploy are separate explicit operator actions, not part of proof-run automation.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/swarms/proof-runs starts mock proof run.
GET /api/swarms/proof-runs/:id returns status, counts, evidence refs and missing evidence.
POST /api/swarms/proof-runs/:id/rollback performs scoped rollback.
npm scripts exist for proof and rollback.
API tests cover auth, permission, start, inspect and rollback.

body.objective: Add Mission Control proof output so the dashboard shows live proof-run counts, required minimums, missing evidence, links and rollback state.
constraints: Dashboard is not source of truth.
Missing evidence must be shown as blocked.
Registry, prepared leases and active execution stay separate.
No optimistic passed label without required counts.
acceptance_criteria: Mission Control includes latest proof run id, status, counts, required counts and missing evidence.
Dashboard links to goal, loop, leases, claims, panel, traces, checkpoints, manifests and memory candidate.
Dashboard smoke verifies proof section and blocked state.
Active execution labels still require runtime evidence.

body.objective: Run the live workstation mock proof smoke and capture pre-counts, post-counts, authenticated API output, dashboard proof and rollback evidence.
constraints: Use workstation DB/API as source of truth.
Do not use in-memory tests as proof of live output.
Capture rollback evidence.
Do not touch unrelated dirty worktree files.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-run counts capture current zero-state for required tables.
Post-run counts meet every proof-contract minimum.
Authenticated API returns same proof run id and counts.
Dashboard proof smoke passes.
Rollback returns proof-run demo records to zero without deleting unrelated records.

body.objective: Run bounded Codex and OpenCode proof smokes after mock proof passes, or record explicit runtime blocked reasons.
constraints: Mock proof must pass first.
Real runtime smokes remain low-risk and bounded.
Unavailable, locked or drifted runtime is a valid blocked result only with evidence.
Proof-run output may auto-create rollback-scoped demo records; release actions remain explicit operator commands.
acceptance_criteria: Codex smoke records runtime contract, stdout, stderr, artifacts, usage or unknown usage, trace spans and checkpoints.
OpenCode smoke records the same or an explicit blocked reason.
Wall-clock, timeout and kill behavior are recorded.
Mission Control distinguishes mock proof from real runtime smoke.

body.objective: Create the sellable demo pack: runbook, proof summary, screenshots/API evidence, caveats, rollback proof and go/no-go checklist.
constraints: Do not market unsupported functionality as active.
Clearly separate mock proof, real runtime smoke and future external SDK integrations.
Include caveats for Ruflo inspiration and registry-versus-execution truth.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook contains exact commands and expected output shapes.
Proof summary shows before/after counts and dashboard/API evidence.
Caveats are explicit and non-defensive.
External reviewer can reproduce the proof run and rollback.
Go/no-go checklist is complete.
